title: Day is dying in the west
firstLine: Day is dying in the west,
publicDomainBasis: Mary Artemesia Lathbury (1841–1913), William Fiske Sherwin (1826–1888). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Day is dying in the west,
Heav’n is touching earth with rest,
Wait and worship while the night
Sets her evening lamps alight
Through all the sky.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Lord of life, beneath the dome
Of the universe, Thy home,
Gather us, who seek Thy face,
To the fold of Thy embrace,
For Thou art nigh.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
While the deepening shadows fall,
Heart of love, enfolding all,
Through the glory and the grace
Of the stars that veil Thy face,
Our hearts ascend.
